summary: PR 2360 focused on matching and naming the Records Misc mncount menu. It replaced many address-style mncount, gm, gmMainLib, and trophy-total symbols with semantic names, added typed mncount row/user-data definitions, implemented most mncount behavior, and propagated renamed helpers through callers. The only loaded feedback is the decomp bot report showing +1724 matched-code bytes, 4 new matches, and 10 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/mn/mncount.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching target; adds mncount menu implementation, typed row helpers, text creation, input handling, and user-data setup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.c adds mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_Create

key_files.role: Header updated with MnCountData layout, MNCOUNT_VISIBLE_ROWS, mnCount_row enum, GET_MNCOUNT macro, and recovered prototypes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.h replaces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and address-style prototypes with typed signatures

diff_excerpt: symbols.txt renames gm_8016279C to gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal and un_80304870 to un_GetTrophyTotal

Header prototypes synchronized with renamed gmMainLib accessors.

Trophy list callers updated to un_GetTrophyTotal.

what_changed: Renamed multiple address-style symbols to semantic names in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, including mnCount_GetMatchTime, mnCount_GetKOKingpin, mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators, mnCount_InitUserData, mnCount_Create, gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal, gm_GetPlayTime, gm_IncrementPowerCount, gmMainLib_Get* accessors, and un_GetTrophyTotal.

Reworked src/melee/mn/mncount.c from a small partially typed file into a larger menu implementation using typed persistent fighter fields such as play_time, sd_count, victories, losses, damage_dealt, and damage_taken.
Introduced mnCount_row enum values for Records Misc rows such as POWER_COUNT, VS_PLAY_TIME, TROPHY_TOTAL, LONGEST_TIME, SMASH_CHAMP, KO_KINGPIN, and DISASTER_MASTER.
Added helper functions for character-row and numeric-row value retrieval: mnCount_GetRowValue_Character and mnCount_GetRowValue_Number.
Added row rendering through mnCount_CreateRow, including label/value text allocation, time formatting, character-name rendering, and fallback dash output for invalid character rows.
Added menu input handling via mnCount_HandleUserInput for back/up/down navigation and scroll updates.
Added arrow indicator update logic in mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators, including explicit comment that GET_JOBJ breaks this access shape.
Added MnCountData fields for scroll_pos, expand_anim_duration, labels, values, and title; initialized them in mnCount_InitUserData.
Updated headers to remove several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations and expose recovered mncount/gmMainLib/gm signatures.
Updated cross-file call sites in gm, mn, ty, if, and lb areas to use the renamed functions.
Bot report recorded 4 new matches in main/melee/mn/mncount: mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_8025072C, .bss, and mnCount_8025035C.

Bot report recorded 10 improvements in unmatched mncount items, including mnCount_8025092C, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, fn_80251640, mnCount_Create, mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, fn_802514D8, .sdata2, .sdata, and .data.
smart_moves: Semantic renames were propagated through symbols.txt, source definitions, headers, and callers rather than hidden behind aliases.
The PR replaced a local opaque LocalFighterData struct with existing typed persistent fighter data fields where the diff shows known field names.
Rows were modeled as a named enum, making switch dispatch in row-value and row-classification helpers easier to review than raw numeric row IDs.
Shared row classification was factored into small inline helpers, inline_is_row_time and inline_is_row_char, instead of duplicating row-category logic at every render site.
Recovered signatures were reflected in headers, including function-pointer argument types for ranking helpers and bool mode parameters.
The PR kept some address-style names for functions that were not semantically recovered, rather than forcing every symbol into a guessed name.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a menu screen is driven by a fixed list of UI rows, a named enum plus row-classification helpers can turn raw row IDs into reviewable source and make value dispatch explicit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.h adds typedef enum mnCount_row with POWER_COUNT through DISASTER_MASTER
raw_diff: mncount.c adds inline_is_row_time and inline_is_row_char

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a recovered body proves both a signature and call-site semantics, update the owning header and call sites together instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or address-style prototypes behind.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.h replaces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for mnCount_8025092C, mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_HandleUserInput, and others
diff_excerpt: gm_1601.h and gmmain_lib.h prototypes renamed and synchronized

decomp_lessons.lesson: For record/stat accessors, semantic names can be supported by the field returned and by repeated call-site usage, but unsupported helpers can remain address-style until meaning is clearer.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmMainLib_GetVsPlayTime returns &gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.x1A34 and callers use it in play-time calculations

decomp_lessons.lesson: Typed data access improves clarity over temporary local overlay structs when project types expose the relevant fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed LocalFighterData and replaced uses with GetPersistentFighterData(i)->play_time, ->sd_count, ->victories, ->losses, ->damage_dealt, ->damage_taken

naming_conventions.convention: mncount public helpers use mnCount_ plus verb/object names, e.g. mnCount_GetMatchTime, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators, mnCount_InitUserData, mnCount_Create.
Game-mode record stat accessors use gmMainLib_Get* for gmMainLib-backed stat pointers and gm_Get* or gm_Increment* for higher-level gm helpers.
Recovered mncount row enum constants use all-caps descriptive names matching Records Misc labels, with comments preserving display text.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.h enum values POWER_COUNT, COMBINED_VS_PLAY_TIME, NO_DEFENSE_NELLY with comments

naming_conventions.convention: A typed user-data accessor macro GET_MNCOUNT is used for MnCountData extraction from HSD_GObj user data.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was retained/introduced narrowly in matching-sensitive functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tylist excerpt shows PAD_STACK(8) near un_803148E4 context
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No reviewer explanation is loaded; this should be treated as tactic evidence only, not a general style endorsement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Noinline wrapper functions were added around functions that otherwise may inline differently.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static void mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators_noinline calls mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators
raw_diff: static void mnCount_InitUserData_noinline calls mnCount_InitUserData
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The diff supports that wrappers exist; no loaded comment proves why they were required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Manual inline expansion was used in fn_802514D8 and fn_80251640 for text cleanup and arrow-indicator logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: comments '// inline_free_text' and expanded label/value freeing loops
raw_diff: comment '// mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators' followed by expanded JObj flag logic
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is matching-tactic evidence; standards require objdiff/build justification before treating it as accepted style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct HSD_JObj access was kept where a comment says GET_JOBJ breaks the match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators has 'HSD_JObj* jobj = gobj->hsd_obj; // GET_JOBJ breaks this'
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The comment is evidence of local matching constraint, but no objdiff details are loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch dispatch was used for row categories and row values instead of large generated branch ladders.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This aligns with canonical control-flow standards but the PR slice does not include failed alternatives.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use row enums and typed dispatch for menu record screens
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2360 supports a candidate lesson that fixed menu/stat rows can be modeled as a named enum with helper predicates and switch-based value dispatch, improving reviewability while matching mncount.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.c adds inline_is_row_time, inline_is_row_char, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, mnCount_GetRowValue_Character

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record-stat accessors named by stat meaning
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2360 supports a candidate naming observation that record/stat accessors were renamed from address-style names to semantic Get* names when their field and call-site role were clear.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Noinline wrappers and manual inline expansions appeared in mncount matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2360 contains noinline wrapper helpers and manually expanded inline blocks in mncount. These should be indexed as tactics observed in this PR, not as blanket recommendations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: expanded inline_free_text and mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators blocks

source_update_candidates.title: mncount owns Records Misc count menu implementation
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/mn/mncount.c implements the Records Misc count menu entry path: mnmain calls mnCount_Create for SEL_RECORDS_MISC, mnCount_Create sets cur_menu to MENU_KIND_RECORDS_MISC, allocates MnCountData, creates text/title rows, and installs mnCount_HandleUserInput.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnCount_Create sets MENU_KIND_RECORDS_MISC and sets up procs
raw_diff: mncount.h defines MnCountData and mnCount_Create prototype
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path lookup/classification for Records Misc menu work.

source_update_candidates.title: mncount row taxonomy
source_update_candidates.text: mnCount_row enumerates 30 Records Misc rows from POWER_COUNT through DISASTER_MASTER, with the first 20 treated as numeric/time rows and later rows including character-based records such as LONGEST_TIME, SMASH_CHAMP, KO_KINGPIN, NO_DEFENSE_NELLY, and DISASTER_MASTER.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mncount.h adds typedef enum mnCount_row
raw_diff: inline_is_row_time and inline_is_row_char classify rows
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for future search and understanding of record-row value dispatch.

source_update_candidates.title: Index mncount-specific matching tactics with caution
source_update_candidates.text: Observed tactics include PAD_STACK(4), noinline wrapper helpers, direct gobj->hsd_obj access with comment 'GET_JOBJ breaks this', and manually expanded inline_free_text/arrow-indicator code. These should remain PR-specific tactic evidence unless curator finds verifier evidence beyond this slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: noinline wrappers and manual inline blocks
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards require explicit objdiff/build evidence for such tactics; this PR provides observed examples but not enough for a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all noinline wrappers or manual inline expansions are accepted style; this slice only proves they were used here.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale from merge status; there are no loaded human review comments.
Do not promote the semantic function names as independently verified game semantics beyond the fields/call sites shown in this PR.
Do not claim full mncount completion; the bot report still lists several unmatched items as improvements rather than new matches.
